title: Pattern Hitting Times
description: Learn probability by playing with it: turn pattern-matching questions into states, transitions, and recurrences — race patterns, walk to ruin, consolidate the fundamentals, then close with a one-line overlap shortcut and its fair-game proof. Optional "For the interview" notes carry the quant framing.
persona: For anyone curious about probability who likes to learn by doing — no prior probability, statistics, or algebra assumed. Quant-interview prep is a fully-served optional track, not a prerequisite to walk in.

lessons.title: Watch the First Heads
lessons.summary: Optional warm-up: flip until the first heads and see why E[H] = 2 — the gentle on-ramp to the flagship.

lessons.title: Pattern Hitting Times
lessons.summary: The flagship: compare HH against HT and prove why E[HH] = 6 but E[HT] = 4.

lessons.title: Penney's Game
lessons.summary: Two patterns race on one stream — who appears first is decoupled from expected wait, and going second can win 7 to 1.

lessons.title: Gambler's Ruin
lessons.summary: The same first-step machine on a number line: P(ruin) = i/N and E[duration] = i(N−i); a tiny edge is catastrophic.

lessons.title: Mixed Review & Streaks
lessons.summary: An interleaved checkpoint: mix waiting, racing, and walking so you can tell the tools apart the way a real problem arrives — unlabeled.

lessons.title: Longer Patterns & Overlap
lessons.summary: Transfer the method to the novel contrast THH vs HTH (E[THH] = 8, E[HTH] = 10) the long way, with faded hints.

lessons.title: The Overlap Shortcut
lessons.summary: Capstone: read E[wait] = Σ 2^(overlap length) straight off the pattern, proved by a fair-casino martingale — re-derives 6, 4, 8, 10 in one line.

roadmap.title: Weighted Coins & Dice
roadmap.summary: Generalize the method beyond fair binary flips to biased coins and larger alphabets.
